name: Italian Republic (1802–1805)
description: A red field bearing a white square set diagonally as a lozenge (diamond), within which sits a smaller upright green square — the 'carre' design.
history: Adopted on 20 August 1802 when the Cisalpine Republic was renamed the Italian Republic under Napoleon Bonaparte as president, replacing the earlier vertical green-white-red tricolour of the Cisalpine. The geometric rearrangement of the three Italian colours is generally credited to vice-president Francesco Melzi d'Eril. The design was used until the Republic became the Napoleonic Kingdom of Italy in 1805. The presidential standard of the modern Italian Republic, adopted in 2000, was inspired by this flag.

features.symbolism: the white of the Italian tricolour, rotated into a diamond enclosing the green square
the green of the Italian tricolour
